title: Sustainable Architecture
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Sustainable architecture has (0) emerged as a crucial response to environmental challenges facing our planet. Architects are increasingly (1) turning to eco-friendly materials and energy-efficient designs. Green buildings aim to reduce their environmental (2) footprint by using renewable energy sources and minimising waste. Solar panels and rainwater collection systems are now commonly (3) incorporated into new constructions. These features not only benefit the environment but also (4) cut down on energy costs for building owners. The concept of passive housing, which (5) relies on natural heating and cooling, has gained popularity in many countries. Some architects are even (6) looking into using recycled materials to construct entire buildings. Cities around the world are (7) putting pressure on developers to meet strict environmental standards. As climate change concerns grow, sustainable architecture will undoubtedly (8) take on an even more important role in shaping our urban landscapes for future generations.

example.explanation: 'Emerged as' indica que algo se ha desarrollado hasta convertirse en algo importante.

questions.explanation: 'Turning to' significa recurrir a algo como solución.
'Environmental footprint' es la expresión estándar para el impacto ambiental.
'Incorporated into' significa integrar algo como parte de un todo.
'Cut down on' significa reducir la cantidad de algo.
'Relies on' indica depender de algo para funcionar.
'Looking into' significa investigar o explorar una posibilidad.
'Putting pressure on' es la colocación correcta para ejercer presión.
'Take on' significa asumir o adquirir una característica o responsabilidad.
